channel.title: Deals on Mizubikes
channel.description: Recent content in Deals on Mizubikes

item.title: Balance Bike vs Training Wheels: What's Best for a 2-Year-Old?
item.description:
Balance bike vs training wheels—it’s one of the first big decisions you’ll face when buying your toddler’s first bike. If your little one is around 2 years old and ready to ride, you’ve probably wondered which option will help them build confidence, balance, and coordination.

As a parent going through this decision right now for my own daughter, I spent time researching both. Here’s what I found—and which style we’re going with.

item.title: Beginner Bike Repair Tools: The Essential Starter Kit for DIY Bike Maintenance
item.description:
Learning to fix your bike at home is easier than you think. With a few beginner bike repair tools, you can save money, skip unnecessary trips to the shop, and gain confidence in your bike maintenance skills.

Whether you’re a new cyclist or just want to be more self-sufficient on the road or trail, this essential starter kit includes everything you need to tackle the most common bike repairs yourself.

item.title: New SRAM Eagle 70 and 90 vs Shimano SLX, XT, XTR: Full Drivetrain Comparison with Weight and Price
item.description: If you’re shopping for a new mechanical drivetrain in 2024, you’ve likely come across the new SRAM Eagle 70 and Eagle 90. These newly released groupsets bring modern mechanical shifting to a more accessible price point—fitting in just between SRAM’s GX and SX lines. But how do they compare to Shimano’s popular SLX, XT, and premium XTR drivetrains?

item.title: Unboxing the 2025 Specialized Stumpjumper 15 Alloy – Is This the Best Trail Frame Deal Right Now?
item.description:
Today I’m unboxing a frame I’ve been genuinely excited to try — the 2025 Specialized Stumpjumper 15 Alloy.

The real kicker? It’s on sale right now, marked down from $2299 to $1499 for the frame and shock. At this price point, it’s easily one of the best values in the trail bike world today. To be honest, I’ve always had a hard time justifying alloy frames priced north of $2000, so this discount made the decision much easier.

item.title: Shimano Dura-Ace vs. Ultegra R8100: Is the Weight Savings Worth the Cost?
item.description:
If you’re debating whether to upgrade from Shimano Ultegra R8100 Di2 to Dura-Ace R9200 Di2, you’re probably wondering: Is the weight savings worth the extra cost? In this post, we break down the weight differences for each component, the cost per gram saved, and whether Dura-Ace delivers the best bang for your buck.

Weight and Cost Comparison: Dura-Ace R9200 vs. Ultegra R8100

Shimano’s Dura-Ace groupset is its top-tier offering, boasting lighter materials, refined shifting, and marginal gains in performance. Meanwhile, Ultegra R8100 delivers similar performance but at a lower price point, making it an attractive choice for many riders.

item.title: Top Deals on Bike Parts for February 11, 2025 – Upgrade Your Ride for Less!
item.description:
Continental Grand Prix 5000 S TR 700x28 Black - Tubeless Ready - Pack of 2 Tires - (Expired)

The Continental Grand Prix 5000 S TR 700x28 is one of the best tubeless-ready tires on the market, known for its excellent balance of speed, durability, and puncture resistance. Whether you’re riding for performance or endurance, these tires deliver.

Some key reasons why these are a great buy:
✅ Low rolling resistance – Smooth and efficient ride
✅ Puncture protection – Great for everyday use
✅ Lightweight – Keeps your bike fast and responsive
✅ Tubeless-ready – Better grip and comfort at lower pressures

item.title: Cannondale CAAD 13: Is Aluminum still a good value vs the Carbon 2023 SuperSix Evo Hi-Mod?
item.description:
CAAD 13 aluminum vs. 2023 SuperSix Evo Hi-Mod carbon cost comparison

Cannondale has always continued to make quality aluminum bikes for as long as I can remember, even after carbon took over the top step and the CAAD 13 is no exception. The question is, is aluminum still a good value with all the reasonably priced carbon options out there?

Just comparing the framesets

The CAAD 13 framesets can be found for around $1000-$1400 and I’ve seen some go for even cheaper used. That usually includes a frame, fork, headset, and a seatpost. Comparing this to the new Cannondale 2023 SuperSix Evo Hi-Mod which goes for $4500 new.
